topic: Two-zone disc topology — V13 fitted parameter, ~600× worse than standard geodesy
change_summary: detail_verdict_length grew from 1320 to 2908 (+120.3%); detail_verdict_text now incorporates Clarke 1866/Bessel 1841 historical-baseline sentence (ISS-1882, applied 2026-05-08T13:16) and AIC/BIC information-criterion defender-pivot paragraph (EXP-329 deep-attack, integrated 2026-05-14T01:22). tldr_verdict_length 163→168 (+5 chars) reflects ISS-1883 sub-meter→sub-centimeter fix. Compared on full PROP-019 schema. Sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-16T01:10Z; obe_skipped=4 (WIN-052/054/055/033 already re-reviewed since audit). Net change is substantive defense preemption, not bloat.
our_argument_summary: WIN-057 (verdict: Misleading) attacks V13 two-zone disc topology on three grounds: (1) equatorial ring radius 14,105 km is a free parameter fitted to six WGS84 city-pair distances, not derived from dome geometry; (2) cross-equatorial RMSE improved from 25–78% to 6.2% but NH RMSE regressed from 5.2% to 7.3%; (3) 6.2% RMSE remains ~600× worse than Vincenty/WGS84's sub-0.01%, and the model still excludes near-equatorial cities below ±12° (OPEN-015). After c4 patches and EXP-329 integration, the verdict text now also preempts (a) the iterative-refinement / Clarke 1866 defense and (b) the AIC/BIC information-criterion defender pivot via a three-pronged response: AICc small-sample failure at n=6/k=4, asymptotic-regime mismatch with OPEN-015 stipulation, and the candidate-set-vs-absolute-performance distinction against WGS84.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: WIN-057 is not monitored automatically — monitor.py has no domain for it. The dome's V13 two-zone topology is documented in the dome site narrative (predictions page, V12→V13 changelog) and verified offline by the author against six city-pair distances. The 14,105 km equatorial ring radius is a static numerical parameter; the dome reports residuals on six known routes.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: There is no automated comparison: the dome states that V13 reduces cross-equatorial RMSE from 25–78% to 6.2% as a narrative claim. The numerical parameter (equatorial ring radius) is a static value derived from a fit to the validation set.
data_sources_used: WGS84 city-pair distances (six routes used as validation set)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: OPEN-015 explicitly excludes near-equatorial cities below ~12° latitude from the model's domain. The 6.2% RMSE figure does not include any cross-equatorial route shorter than the showcase set.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. Our review correctly characterizes the absence of automated monitoring and the curve-fitting nature of the equatorial ring parameter.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no domain, no fetch, and no comparison logic for WIN-057. The V13 topology validation is narrative-only on the dome site.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics (WGS84/Vincenty) provides a complete, sub-mm-accurate global model with no exclusion zone. The dome's V13 two-zone topology is a different geometric framework, not a renaming of the standard model — but it is not a separate physical prediction either; it is curve-fitting on the same data WGS84 already covers globally to sub-cm.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: The 14,105 km equatorial ring radius is fitted to six known WGS84 city-pair distances. The V13 framework was published after these distances were known (WGS84 is the validation set). Trivially post-hoc.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: The numerical parameter (14,105 km) is value-fit to globe data, not derived from dome geometric equations (disc radius 20,015 km, firmament height function H(r)=H₀·exp(−r/λ)). The V13 framework is dome-stipulated but the predicted distance values are not derivable from dome parameters alone — they require the fitted equatorial ring radius. Consistent with WIN-027 and WIN-069 (post-c4 flip via ISS-1881).

holes_found.description: AIC/BIC paragraph contains a conceptual conflation: 'When the model excludes a slice of its prospective domain by stipulation (OPEN-015, cities within ±12°), that out-of-sample regime never arrives, and the asymptotic justification for the complexity penalty has no purchase.' This blurs two distinct concepts: (a) sample size (n) for which AIC's asymptotic justification holds, and (b) the domain of applicability (lat ≥ 12°) over which out-of-sample predictions can be tested. A careful technical defender will note that within the restricted domain (lat ≥ 12°), AIC's asymptotic-loss justification applies normally — the OPEN-015 exclusion just restricts the target population, it does not eliminate the out-of-sample regime within that population. The argument is rhetorically effective but technically overstated. The other two prongs of the rebuttal (AICc small-sample failure at n=6/k=4, and candidate-set-vs-absolute-performance) carry the real load.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the asymptotic-regime sentence to one of two framings: (a) Drop the OPEN-015 entanglement and use the cleaner statement: 'AIC/BIC penalties are derived asymptotically and quantify expected loss on new data from the same distribution; at n=6 the asymptotic regime is reached for neither V13 nor WGS84, and small-sample-aware criteria (AICc, leave-one-out CV) are the only defensible choice — and AICc has already collapsed at this n/k ratio.' (b) Keep OPEN-015 but reframe it as a domain restriction that narrows the population over which the IC argument applies: 'OPEN-015 narrows V13's prospective domain to lat ≥ ±12°; even on that restricted domain, the n=6 sample makes asymptotic IC reasoning unreliable, and AICc fails as shown above.' Either fix preserves the rebuttal's force while removing the conceptual blur a technical defender could exploit.

holes_found.description: AICc edge-of-definability framing could be sharper. The verdict says 'the n/k ratio is below the regime in which information-criterion selection is reliable at all' — true but generic. At n=k+2=6 and k=4, the AICc denominator (n−k−1) is exactly 1; the correction is one step from being mathematically undefined (it diverges at n=k+1=5). A reader who computes the arithmetic sees '40 penalty units' as a large number but may not recognize it as the minimum-well-defined regime of the correction itself. A one-clause addition would harden this against handwaving from a defender ('40 is just a number; AIC can absorb it').
holes_found.recommendation: Add a parenthetical after the AICc calculation. Suggested wording: 'AICc at n = 6, k = 4 adds 2k(k + 1)/(n − k − 1) = 40 penalty units (and is one step from being mathematically undefined — AICc diverges at n = k + 1) — an arithmetic signal...' Eight-word insertion; preserves the existing sentence structure.

stronger_arguments.description: If the dome's V13 documentation is publicly available, the count and identity of the validation routes (six city pairs) can be verified directly. Cite the dome's V13 changelog or section identifier to anchor the 'fitted to six known WGS84 city-pair distances' claim. The current text states the count parenthetically; an explicit citation to the dome's own V13 narrative would close the 'how do we know n=6' question.
sources: dome predictions page V13 changelog / WIN-057 evidence section

stronger_arguments.description: Vincenty/WGS84 absolute accuracy is conservatively stated as 'sub-0.01%' in our text, but Vincenty's analytic accuracy is sub-millimeter (relative error 10⁻⁷ to 10⁻⁹ on the WGS84 ellipsoid for non-antipodal routes). 'Sub-0.01%' understates the gap by 4–6 orders of magnitude. The current 600× ratio is computed on the conservative figure; we could note that the true ratio is 10,000× to 1,000,000× depending on baseline length, then choose the conservative 600× figure for the headline. This pre-empts a defender who points out our chosen comparison floor is itself loose.
sources: Vincenty 1975, Survey Review 23(176):88 — sub-millimeter analytic accuracy on WGS84 ellipsoid

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome's V13 two-zone topology IS a real geometric refinement over V12 on the six routes it was fitted to: 25–78% cross-equatorial RMSE down to 6.2% is a meaningful residual reduction in absolute terms (data/wins.json #057 detail_evidence). The author also publicly acknowledges OPEN-015 (the ±12° exclusion zone) rather than hiding it, and OPEN-001 (the WGS84-input dependency) — these are honest acknowledgments of model limitations that many post-hoc fits would conceal. The kernel here is 'iterative model refinement is normal science' — pointing to V12→V13 as evidence of methodological development is fair.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Iterative refinement is normal science only when each iteration narrows the residual without carving out exclusion zones, and when the parameter additions remain calibrated against out-of-sample data. V13 adds a free parameter fitted to its own validation set (data/wins.json #057 detail_evidence: 'fitted to minimize residuals on six known city-pair distances') and worsens NH accuracy (5.2%→7.3%) — a net regression. Compare Clarke 1866 / Bessel 1841: they reached <0.5% on continental baselines without exclusion zones (data/wins.json #057 detail_verdict_text). The dome's V13 fails this benchmark on both axes — it is 12× worse than 1866 geodesy and excludes ~18% of the global population (OPEN-015, data/wins.json #057 detail_extra). The kernel-of-truth defense survives at defense_survives=2 (preempted in current text), not 4.

deeper_analysis: The c4→c5 trajectory on WIN-057 is the cleanest 'patches landed, verdict strengthens' run in the recent reviewer-stream record. Four c4 holes (one moderate-cross-WIN, one moderate-defender-pivot, two minor-text) have been addressed by three patches and one Opus-class deep-attack EXP: ISS-1881 flipped WIN-069's derives_from_dome to false with a documented cross-WIN evidence note (closing the WIN-027/057/069 trio's internal inconsistency); ISS-1882 added the Clarke 1866/Bessel 1841 historical-baseline sentence; ISS-1883 corrected the TLDR sub-meter→sub-centimeter; and EXP-329 added a ~1450-character three-pronged AIC/BIC rebuttal preempting the academic defender's parameter-fitting-symmetry argument. The only c4 hole not filed as an ISS was hole #4 (retrodiction-jargon gloss, marked 'low priority — optional' by c4); it remains unaddressed and continues to be optional. The 120% drift in detail_verdict_length is therefore signal, not noise — a substantive defense-preemption expansion, not bloat. The c5 review surfaces two new minor weaknesses: a conceptual conflation between sample-size and domain-restriction in the AIC asymptotic-justification sentence (the third sentence of the EXP-329 paragraph), and a missed opportunity to sharpen the AICc-edge-of-definability point. Neither weakens the verdict; both are tightening recommendations. Net assessment: WIN-057 is in stronger shape than at any prior cycle, and the verdict text now successfully preempts the three main fair-minded defender pivots (WGS84-also-fit symmetry, iterative-refinement Clarke baseline, and AIC/BIC information-criterion adjudication).

citations_verified: Vincenty 1975 DOI 10.1179/sre.1975.23.176.88 (Survey Review) — present in detail_evidence, sub-millimeter analytic accuracy claim is widely-documented standard geodesy
Dome predictions page (https://john09289.github.io/predictions) — author definition link in detail_verdict_text
OPEN-015 / OPEN-001 references — present in dome's own narrative per detail_extra
citations_unchecked: AIC/BIC formal definitions and Hurvich-Tsai 1989 AICc derivation — formula '2k(k+1)/(n-k-1)' is the standard small-sample correction; arithmetic at k=4, n=6 verified to equal 40 (see quantitative_verification)

advocate_mode.best_defense: I am a technically-trained dome defender reading the post-EXP-329 verdict text. The reviewers have closed three of the four open defenses (WGS84-was-also-fit symmetry, iterative-refinement-from-Clarke-1866, and AIC/BIC information-criterion adjudication). My remaining angle is the asymptotic-regime sentence, which conflates two distinct concepts. 'AIC/BIC penalties are derived asymptotically: they quantify expected loss when the chosen model is applied to NEW data drawn from the same distribution. When the model excludes a slice of its prospective domain by stipulation (OPEN-015, cities within ±12°), that out-of-sample regime never arrives, and the asymptotic justification for the complexity penalty has no purchase.' This is technically wrong on its own terms. OPEN-015 restricts the DOMAIN of the model (lat ≥ ±12°). It does not eliminate the OUT-OF-SAMPLE regime — there are billions of out-of-sample points within lat ≥ 12° on which V13 could be tested. The asymptotic justification for AIC works perfectly well on a restricted target population; it does not require global coverage. The reviewers are conflating 'domain restriction' (which is fine) with 'sample-size failure' (which is what AICc actually flags). The third prong (candidate-set vs absolute performance) DOES kill my AIC defense, but the second prong is technically loose, and a careful reader will notice. Beyond that, my remaining angle is the absolute-accuracy comparison: the reviewers compute the 600× ratio using a 'sub-0.01%' figure for Vincenty, but Vincenty is actually sub-mm — they have understated the gap by 4–6 orders of magnitude. They've chosen the conservative comparison to make my position look better, but a reader who follows the citation will see they've actually underclaimed the gap.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: The asymptotic-regime conflation is the only remaining angle a fair-minded technical defender can credibly press. Address it by either dropping the OPEN-015 entanglement from that sentence (the strongest move) or reframing OPEN-015 as a domain restriction that narrows but does not eliminate the AIC-justifying regime (the safer move that preserves the original argument's rhetorical structure). See holes_found item 1 for two specific replacement-text candidates. This is a minor edit (one sentence) but it would close the last defender-pivot angle. The 'Vincenty is actually sub-mm not sub-0.01%' point is even better — adopting a sharper comparison floor strengthens the headline ratio without changing the verdict; see stronger_arguments item 2.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: Post-c4 the WIN-027/057/069 detail_group trio is now internally consistent: all three carry post_hoc=true and derives_from_dome=false (data/wins.json #027, #057, #069 code_analysis fields). WIN-069's flipped derives_from_dome carries a documented cross-WIN evidence note referencing ISS-1881 and WIN-057.c4. No outstanding inconsistencies in this trio. Spot-check against WIN-068 (any other Australia-related distance claim) and WIN-056 (other long-distance / coordinate-system WINs): no detected conflict — those WINs are differently scoped and do not contradict the V13 critique.

claims_checked: AICc small-sample correction at k=4, n=6: 2k(k+1)/(n-k-1) = 40 penalty units
AICc edge-of-definability: n=k+1 gives denominator=0 (undefined); n=k+2 gives denominator=1 (minimum well-defined)
Parameter count: dome H₀, λ, EW=0.9941, equatorial_ring=14,105 km → 4 free parameters; WGS84 a=6,378,137 m, f=1/298.257 → 2 parameters
OPEN-015 population estimate: ±12° tropical band excludes ~1.5 billion people (~18-20% of global population)
quantitative_verification.computations: (1) AICc correction: verified arithmetically (40 = 2*4*5/1). The 'one step from undefined' framing is exact: at n=5 the denominator is 0. (2) 600× ratio: 6.2/0.01 = 620, so 'over 600×' is correct and conservative. The true gap is larger if Vincenty's sub-mm analytic accuracy is used as the comparison floor (relative error 10⁻⁷ → 6.2/10⁻⁵ = 6.2×10⁵ on percent-basis at 1000 km route, i.e. 620,000× — but our review uses the conservative 0.01% floor to avoid being challenged on Vincenty's idealized accuracy vs achievable accuracy with realistic data). (3) Parameter count: confirmed against detail_evidence.
